context.description: The page argues that human judgment is strongly shaped by biological instincts, especially sexual drive, and then turns to the question of how information becomes conscious. It introduces the section "The Source of Light" and contrasts the body as a filtering mechanism with awareness as the receiver of experience.
normalization_notes: Section heading translated from "Die Quelle des Lichts".

rows.paragraph_text: The author argues that people are heavily influenced by biology and sexual instinct, then shifts to the question of how information becomes conscious.
rows.text_content: Human judgment is shaped by biological instinct.

rows.paragraph_text: Consciousness receives filtered information from the body, which is presented as a machine, and the page returns to the mirror-based example as a way to examine awareness.
rows.text_content: Awareness receives information filtered through the body.

content.free_text: book_page nonfiction_philosophy philosophy_of_consciousness Nonfiction philosophy page on consciousness, biology, instinct, and awareness. Section heading: The Source of Light. Page 38 in Bewusstseinsreisen - Version 2.pdf.
